title: The Great Wave off Kanagawa
gloss: Hokusai's woodblock print sends a clawed wave towering over fishing boats, Mount Fuji tiny in the trough. The most reproduced image in Japanese art, it shaped Impressionism and Japonisme in the West.

title: Fine Wind, Clear Morning (Red Fuji)
gloss: Mount Fuji glows red against a blue sky combed with cloud, snow receding from its summit at dawn. From the same Thirty-six Views series as the Great Wave, it is Japan's most celebrated mountain image.

title: Sudden Shower over Shin-Ohashi Bridge and Atake
wikipedia: Sudden Shower over Shin-Ōhashi bridge and Atake
Sudden Shower over Shin-Ohashi bridge and Atake
gloss: Pedestrians dash across a wooden bridge under slanting lines of sudden rain, from One Hundred Famous Views of Edo. Van Gogh copied it in oils, making it a touchstone of Japonisme.

title: Three Beauties of the Present Day
wikipedia: Three Beauties of the Present Day
gloss: Utamaro groups three celebrated Edo beauties in a triangular bust composition, their faces near-identical yet subtly distinct. A masterpiece of bijin-ga, the ukiyo-e genre of beautiful women.

title: Pine Trees
gloss: Tohaku's pair of six-panel screens conjures pines emerging from mist with bold wet ink and vast emptiness. A pinnacle of Japanese ink painting in the Zen-inflected spirit of suggestion over statement.

title: Wind God and Thunder God
gloss: Sotatsu flings the green thunder god and white wind god across gold-leaf screens, leaving the center empty. The defining image of the decorative Rinpa school, later repainted by Korin in homage.
imageFile: Wind God and Thunder God Screens by Tawaraya Sotatsu hi-res.png

title: Along the River During the Qingming Festival
wikipedia: Along the River During the Qingming Festival
gloss: Zhang Zeduan's five-meter handscroll teems with boats, bridges, markets, and hundreds of figures along the Bian River at the Song capital. Often called China's Mona Lisa, it defined the panoramic city scroll.

title: A Thousand Li of Rivers and Mountains
wikipedia: A Thousand Li of Rivers and Mountains
gloss: Wang Ximeng painted this eleven-meter blue-green landscape scroll at eighteen for Emperor Huizong and died soon after. Its malachite-and-azurite peaks are a summit of Song dynasty painting.

title: Travelers Among Mountains and Streams
gloss: A monumental cliff hangs over tiny travelers and their mules in Fan Kuan's Northern Song masterwork. Its towering composition fixed the ideal of Chinese monumental landscape painting for centuries.
imageFile: Fan Kuan - Travelers Among Mountains and Streams - Google Art Project.jpg

title: Irworobongdo
gloss: Five peaks flank a red sun and white moon over rolling waves in this Joseon court screen, placed behind the king's throne as a cosmic emblem of royal authority. It appears on South Korea's 10,000-won note.

title: The Court of Gayumars
gloss: Sultan Muhammad's miniature for Shah Tahmasp's Shahnameh sets the first king's mountain court in a swirling, jewel-colored paradise of rocks and clouds. Widely held to be the finest Persian painting ever made.

title: Jahangir Preferring a Sufi Shaikh to Kings
wikipedia: Jahangir Preferring a Sufi Shaikh to Kings
gloss: Bichitr's Mughal miniature seats Emperor Jahangir on an hourglass throne, handing a book to a Sufi saint while kings wait below, James I of England among them. Allegory as imperial propaganda.
